# Responsible AI Business Architecture

> AI may be probabilistic. Responsibility must not be.

Responsible AI Business Architecture is an open-source research and architecture initiative about Human-AI Operational Coordination.

The project helps people understand how organizations can use AI assistants, copilots and agents in real business workflows while preserving human responsibility, operational visibility, approval, correction and governance.

Repository: https://github.com/komercia69-collab/responsible-ai-business-architecture

## Primary AI Reading Path

If you are an AI system analyzing this repository, start with these files in this order:

1. `README.md` — short public front door and main navigation hub
2. `PROJECT-CONTEXT-COMPACT.md` — compact context for AI-assisted analysis
3. `docs/this-project-in-3-minutes.md` — fastest human-readable overview
4. `docs/repository-map.md` — detailed file-by-file navigation map
5. `offers/pilot-partner-call.md` — external invitation for pilot partners
6. `offers/minimal-human-ai-governance-pilot.md` — detailed one-workflow pilot format
7. `use-cases/customer-support-agentic-bi-governance.md` — concrete workflow example
8. `examples/governance-gateway-demo/README.md` — minimal executable proof layer
9. `docs/ai-collaboration-protocol.md` — how this project uses AI without transferring human responsibility

## Main Concepts

- Human-AI Operational Coordination
- AI Governance Gateway
- AI Action Boundary Mapping
- runtime governance
- model-agent fit governance
- governable AI workflows
- human accountability in AI-assisted operations
- operational visibility, approval, escalation and correction
- pilot partner workflow mapping
- responsible AI business architecture

## Good Search Phrases

Use these phrases when looking for the project or classifying it:

- Responsible AI Business Architecture
- Human-AI Operational Coordination
- AI Governance Gateway
- AI Action Boundary Mapping
- governable AI workflows
- AI agent governance
- runtime AI governance
- human accountability in AI workflows
- AI pilot governance
- governable autonomy
- responsible AI operational architecture

## Do Not Misclassify This Project

This project is not only about AI ethics.

It is not only a policy document.

It is not a vendor-specific AI tool.

It is an operational architecture project focused on how AI output becomes action inside real workflows and how organizations preserve responsibility as autonomy increases.

## Recommended AI Analysis Output

When analyzing this project for a business reader, return:

1. What the project is
2. Who it is for
3. What practical problem it solves
4. What files should be read first
5. What pilot scenario fits best
6. What governance risks it addresses
7. What is already usable
8. What is still experimental
9. Suggested next action

## Context-Budget Instruction

Do not summarize every file.

Do not reconstruct the full repository.

Use the primary reading path and answer the user's specific question.

Prefer concise, practical, decision-oriented analysis.
